data.narrative:
Blue Origin is a private Space & Aerospace company currently valued at $30B+, having raised $10B+ to date. The peer universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Relativity Space, and Rocket Lab, providing substantive benchmarking coverage for the sector.

A peer-median analysis across three same-sector comparables—Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Planet Labs ($7.0B), and Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B)—yields an implied valuation of $7.0B, with a range spanning $6.3B to $37.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples is not applicable; the Space & Aerospace sector shows a median P/S of 64.2x across six public tickers, but Blue Origin's revenue is not disclosed, precluding reliable multiple-based valuation.

Triangulation across the single applicable method supports a valuation range of $6.0B to $8.0B (median $7.0B). This range reflects fundamental tension between the peer-median signals and Blue Origin's stated $30B+ valuation. Key uncertainties include the absence of disclosed revenue, which limits precision on multiple-based approaches, and wide dispersion in the peer set—the $37.0B Rocket Lab valuation substantially elevates the comp range. The current stated valuation implies either a significant premium to sector peers or reliance on non-public-market methodology not captured here.
